events.text: Healthy infrastructure starts with a healthy workstation. We scan the local environment for configuration issues -- Node.js version, SSH key presence, and KVM virtualization readiness.
Local environment clear. Now we check the remote machine over SSH. You'll see uptime, disk and memory usage, SMART disk health, container counts, and service status.
To see every running container across all isolated Docker daemons on the machine, we pull a unified view -- with state, health checks, CPU, and memory per container.
Containers checked. Now we query the systemd services running on the machine to see which infrastructure daemons are active and healthy.
For a complete picture, we run a full machine query with no filters -- returning system metrics, network, services, containers, and block devices in one call.
We refresh the SSH host key fingerprints stored in the config for this machine, ensuring future connections can verify the server identity.
Host keys updated. We verify end-to-end connectivity by running a remote command over SSH and confirming the machine's hostname is returned.
One more remote command -- this time we check the machine's uptime and load average, demonstrating arbitrary command execution through the SSH tunnel.
